work.title: Piano Sonata in C Major, K. 545

analysis_source: User-provided form analysis + Schiff Carnegie Hall 2015 timing

sections.note: Primary theme (C major), Secondary theme (G major), then a codetta. The two themes are stated in the tonic and dominant. In Schiff's reading, the entire exposition is repeated.

themes.note: C major opening with a clear eighth-note melody over Alberti bass. A four-bar antecedent and consequent appear twice.
Gentle second theme in the dominant. The two hands engage in a dialogue-like texture.
A short codetta with alternating scales and trills that confirms the cadence of the exposition.

sections.key: g minor, d minor, a minor, C major, a minor, F major
sections.note: A short development moves through g, d, a, C, a, then F major using the primary motive. The final F-major area prepares the unusual subdominant recapitulation.

themes.key: g, d, a, C, a, F
themes.note: Tonal trajectory through six brief regions, ending on F major as the structural pivot.

sections.note: Famously, the recapitulation does not begin in the tonic but in the subdominant (F major). By starting a fifth lower, Mozart can reuse the exposition's modulation pattern so that the secondary theme arrives in the tonic on the second hearing.

themes.note: Subdominant (F major) recapitulation, the central pedagogical point of this movement.
Stated this time in the tonic and heading toward the Essential Structural Closure. The form's core function of unifying both themes in the tonic is finally fulfilled.
Scales and trills return to close the movement.
